action_min_one_toast: ⚠️ Add at least one action before saving.
actions_hint: Actions fire in order when this keyword matches. Your plan allows up to {max} action(s) per trigger.
actions_limit: Your plan allows up to {max} actions per trigger.
actions_limit_toast: ⚠️ Your plan Reached its Max actions per trigger.
conditions_hint:
This trigger only fires if conditions are met.
Use the AND / OR toggle to choose: • AND — every condition must pass • OR — any one condition is enough Conditions can check subscriber tags, referral source, language, custom fields, and more.
conditions_upgrade_toast:
⚠️ Conditional keyword triggers require the Advanced plan.

Advanced unlocks conditions for both keyword triggers and button menus.
cooldown_hint:
Set how long (in seconds) must pass before this keyword can fire again for the same subscriber.
Examples: • 0 — no cooldown, fires every time • 60 — maximum once per minute • 3600 — maximum once per hour • 86400 — maximum once per day Cooldown only affects repeat fires — a subscriber's first match always fires.
cooldown_invalid_msg: Please enter a number between 0 and 86400 (24 hours max).
cooldown_invalid_toast: Please enter a number between 0 and 86400 (24 hours max).
cooldown_upgrade_toast:
⚠️ Keyword cooldown requires the Advanced plan.

Advanced unlocks cooldowns, multi-action triggers, and conditional logic.
delete_confirm: Delete the trigger for {keyword}? This cannot be undone. All associated actions will be removed.
detail_body:
{e_keyword} Keyword: {keyword}
Buttons explained: • Actions — what fires when the keyword matches (messages, menus, forms, tags) • Match Mode — how strictly the keyword is detected in the subscriber's message • Cooldown — minimum time between fires for the same subscriber • Conditions — only fire for subscribers who meet specific criteria • Stats — total number of times this trigger has fired • Group — organise triggers into folders for easier management • Enable / Disable — pause the trigger without deleting it
duplicate_keyword: A trigger for {keyword} already exists. Enter a different keyword:
empty: No keyword triggers set up yet. Tap Add Trigger to create your first one. Triggers can send a message, open a menu, run a form, tag the subscriber, or enroll them in a drip sequence. {e_open_book} How Keywords Work →
fallback_hint:
{e_kw_fallback} Fallback — fires when no other keyword matches the subscriber's message.
Use the fallback to handle unexpected input gracefully — send a help message, open your main menu, or prompt them to try again.
first_trigger_toast: 🎉 First trigger created! Send {keyword} to your bot to test it.
group_hint:
Organise triggers into named folders.
Grouped triggers can be toggled on/off together and are easier to find when you have many triggers. Assign a keyword to a group from its detail page → Group button.
group_new_hint: Group {name} created. Open any keyword trigger and tap Group to assign it.
group_pick_hint: Pick an existing group or create a new one:
group_pick_no_groups: No groups yet — tap New Group to create your first one.
limit_reached_msg:
{e_warning} Keyword Limit Reached You've reached your plan's keyword trigger limit.
Basic — 5 keyword triggers Advanced — unlimited keyword triggers Upgrade your plan to add more.
limit_reached_toast: ⚠️ Keyword limit reached. Upgrade for more.
list_body:
{e_keyword} Keyword Triggers Fire an automatic action when a subscriber types a specific word or phrase — no manual replies needed.
Match modes: • Exact — subscriber types the keyword exactly as written • Contains — keyword appears anywhere in their message • Starts With — their message begins with the keyword • Ends With — their message ends with the keyword • Regex — advanced pattern rules (Business plan) Add a Fallback trigger to catch all unmatched messages. {e_open_book} How Keywords Work →
list_count: You have {count} active trigger(s). Tap any to edit.
match_ends_upgrade_toast:
⚠️ Ends With matching requires the Advanced plan.

Advanced unlocks all match types: Contains, Starts With, and Ends With.
match_regex_upgrade_toast:
⚠️ Regex matching requires the Business plan.

Business unlocks regex patterns, geo-targeting polls, unlimited bots, and unlimited keywords.
match_upgrade_toast:
⚠️ Contains and Starts With matching require the Advanced plan.

Advanced unlocks all match types and multi-action triggers. Business adds regex matching.
no_forms_toast: ⚠️ No active forms found. Create one first.
no_menus_toast: ⚠️ No menus found. Create one first.
prompt_match_mode:
Choose a match mode How should this keyword be detected in the subscriber's message?
Tip: Contains is the most flexible — it catches the keyword anywhere in the sentence. Exact avoids false matches on longer messages. {e_open_book} Match modes explained →
prompt_send_message: Enter the message text to send when this keyword matches: HTML formatting is supported: <b>, <i>, <code>, <a href="...">
prompt_sequence_id: Please enter a numeric sequence ID:
regex_explain: {e_kw_regex} Regex Mode Your pattern uses Python re.search() — checks if the pattern appears anywhere in the subscriber's message. Quick examples: • \d+ → any number • (buy|price|order) → any of these words • ^hello → starts with "hello" • \d{10} → exactly 10 consecutive digits {e_open_book} Full regex guide → Enter your pattern below:
regex_invalid: Invalid regex pattern. Error: {error} Please check your pattern and try again.
regex_prompt: Enter your regex pattern (Python re.search syntax): Example: (buy|price|order) matches any of those words anywhere in the message.
stats_all_empty: No keyword triggers have fired yet. Stats appear here as soon as subscribers start using your bot.
stats_never: This trigger has never fired yet. Stats update in real time as subscribers interact with your bot. Test it by sending {keyword} to your bot.
subtitle: Auto-respond when subscribers type a keyword
title: Keyword Triggers
